required_header.download_date: ClinicalTrials.gov processed this data on March 2026
required_header.link_text: Link to the current ClinicalTrials.gov record.

clinical_study.brief_title: Erdafitinib in Locally Advanced or Metastatic Urothelial Carcinoma with FGFR3 Mutation After Platinum Failure in Cisplatin-Ineligible Patients

brief_summary.textblock: This study evaluates erdafitinib (8 mg daily with up-titration) in patients with locally advanced or metastatic high-grade urothelial carcinoma of the bladder harboring FGFR3 activating mutations or fusions, who progressed on or after gemcitabine/carboplatin chemotherapy. The study is specifically designed for cisplatin-ineligible patients (CrCl less than 45 mL/min) with CKD. Hyperphosphatemia management with phosphate binders is a pre-specified component of supportive care. The primary endpoint is objective response rate per RECIST 1.1.

detailed_description.textblock: Patients with histologically confirmed high-grade urothelial carcinoma of the bladder, muscle-invasive or locally advanced/metastatic disease, FGFR3 activating mutation (S249C, R248C, Y373C, or other known activating mutations) or FGFR3 fusion confirmed by NGS are eligible. Cisplatin ineligibility must be documented by CrCl less than 45 mL/min. Prior treatment with gemcitabine/carboplatin (or gemcitabine/cisplatin if previously eligible) is required with documented disease progression. Patients receive erdafitinib 8 mg orally once daily. Phosphate monitoring is performed every 2 weeks; hyperphosphatemia is managed with dietary phosphate restriction and sevelamer. Patients with pre-existing peripheral neuropathy (Grade 1 or less at baseline) and chronic kidney disease (CrCl 30-45 mL/min) are eligible. Baseline ECOG PS 0-2 is permitted.

clinical_study.study_design: Intervention Model: Single Group Assignment, Primary Purpose: Treatment

criteria.textblock:
Inclusion Criteria:
- Histologically confirmed high-grade urothelial carcinoma of the bladder
- Locally advanced (cT4a or higher) or nodal (N1-N3) disease without distant metastases, or metastatic (M1) disease
- FGFR3 activating mutation (S249C, R248C, Y373C, G380R, or equivalent) or FGFR3 fusion confirmed by validated NGS panel
- Cisplatin-ineligible at time of first-line therapy: CrCl less than 45 mL/min by Cockcroft-Gault or equivalent
- Prior gemcitabine/carboplatin chemotherapy (1 line) with documented disease progression
- CKD stage 3 or higher (CrCl 30-45 mL/min) is acceptable
- Pre-existing peripheral neuropathy Grade 1 or less at baseline is permitted
- Benign prostatic hyperplasia (male patients) is permitted
- Age 18 years or older
- ECOG performance status 0, 1, or 2
- Adequate organ function: platelets greater than or equal to 75, hemoglobin greater than or equal to 8 g/dL, AST/ALT less than or equal to 3x ULN, total bilirubin less than or equal to 1.5x ULN
Exclusion Criteria:
- Transitional cell carcinoma of upper tract (renal pelvis or ureter) as primary site
- FGFR3 wildtype tumor (no FGFR3 mutation or fusion)
- Prior FGFR inhibitor therapy (erdafitinib, infigratinib, pemigatinib)
- CrCl less than 30 mL/min (severe CKD or dialysis-dependent)
- Pre-existing hyperphosphatemia greater than 5.5 mg/dL that is unmanageable with dietary restriction
- Active or untreated retinal disorder or central serous retinopathy (erdafitinib ocular toxicity risk)
- Uncontrolled hyponatremia (sodium less than 130 mEq/L) at baseline
- Prior Grade 3 or higher peripheral neuropathy
- Active symptomatic brain metastases
- NSAID use (allergy or intolerance noted; NSAIDs are contraindicated due to GI bleed risk in patients with prior NSAID-related GI events)
- Cisplatin-eligible patients (CrCl greater than or equal to 60 mL/min)
